Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent announced the U.S. will freeze global funds of Iranian leadership and the IRGC, with willingness to apply secondary sanctions on countries purchasing Iranian oil.[1][2] This represents an escalation of economic pressure on Iran beyond previous "maximum pressure" efforts over the past year.[2]

Treasury Secretary Bessent stated the Federal Reserve is "doing the right thing by sitting and watching" to assess how the Iran conflict affects the U.S. economy before cutting rates, marking a reversal from the Trump administration's historical pressure for rate cuts.[4]
